# Security Checklist: Troubleshooting, Security, Trust, Update, And Rollback

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-008 requirements quality for fact-bound security/trust documentation, source separation, platform permission boundaries, and no-overclaim language.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are security/trust requirements complete enough to separate official vendor behavior, repository facts, and recommended practice into distinct reader-visible groups? [Completeness, Spec FR-007, Spec SC-005]
- [x] CHK002 Are required trust surfaces covered, including source tree, generated payloads, installed cache/runtime state, marketplace manifests, skills, hooks, MCP/app integrations, sandboxing, approvals, managed settings or policy, update flow, and rollback boundaries? [Completeness, Spec FR-010]
- [x] CHK003 Are both Claude Code subagents and Codex custom agents included in the security/trust coverage so agent capability claims are not hidden under generic plugin wording? [Completeness, Spec US2, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-015, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK004 Are repository-fact citation requirements defined for manifests, skills, agents, hooks, scripts, tests, generated payloads, and source-vs-dist evidence? [Completeness, Spec FR-009, Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK005 Are managed policy and organization-control scenarios documented as boundaries the user should not bypass? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-012, Spec Key Entities]
- [x] CHK006 Does the spec explicitly state that browser-rendered docs do not grant permissions or run workflows, in addition to not executing local commands, inspecting local filesystems, or modifying configuration? [Security, Spec FR-006]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is the citation policy clear that official vendor docs support only platform-behavior claims, not Racecraft source, generated payload, installed cache, or observed local file-layout claims? [Clarity, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-014]
- [x] CHK008 Are installed cache and cache-mutation requirements phrased so direct cache edits, cache deletion, or cache directory removal cannot be interpreted as default fixes? [Clarity, Spec FR-013, Spec FR-014]
- [x] CHK009 Are read-only inspection requirements specific enough to exclude login, install, remove, reload, restart, approve, edit, set, unset, delete, rebuild, config-write, cache-edit, or token/secret-printing commands from inspect cells? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Spec Key Entities]
- [x] CHK010 Is recommended-practice wording constrained so derived guidance is not presented as a platform guarantee, security attestation, certification, formal threat model, control audit, or verified control finding? [Clarity, Spec Recommended Practice, Spec SC-005]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do Codex requirements consistently preserve the separation between plugin installation, bundled skill loading, marketplace CLI operations, custom-agent registration, MCP/app integrations, managed configuration, and restart? [Consistency, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-015]
- [x] CHK012 Do Claude Code requirements consistently preserve the separation between marketplace update/remove, plugin install/uninstall, reload, detail inspection, managed policy, installed runtime state, hooks, subagents, and cache behavior? [Consistency, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK013 Do troubleshooting, security/trust, and update/rollback requirements use the same source, generated payload, installed runtime/cache, managed policy, approval, and recovery boundaries? [Consistency, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-012]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK014 Are platform-behavior and Racecraft-specific claim coverage outcomes measurable as 100% citation requirements rather than subjective trust language? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-003, Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK015 Is the no-audit/no-certification outcome measurable by requiring an explicit statement that DOC-008 is not a security audit, certification, formal threat model, or control attestation? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-005]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK016 Are stale install and rollback scenarios covered without requiring direct edits to generated payloads or installed caches? [Coverage, Spec US3, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-012, Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK017 Are platform-vendor drift scenarios covered so command names, settings locations, sandbox behavior, approvals, hooks, MCP, agents, and plugin behavior must be rechecked against current official docs before shipping? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-008]
- [x] CHK018 Are permission, approval, sandbox, managed policy, and MCP/config boundaries covered as security-sensitive reader flows rather than generic troubleshooting failures? [Coverage, Spec US2, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-015, Spec FR-016]
